# Marginal Baseline Evaluation

> Agent-ready metric auditing for machine-learning training metrics.

Install: `pip install mbe-eval`

Source: https://github.com/AparajeetS/marginal-baseline-eval

PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/mbe-eval/

Use MBE when a user has a table of training runs and asks whether a metric still
predicts held-out performance after controlling for ordinary baselines.

Machine commands:

- `mbe-eval-demo --bootstrap 200`
- `mbe-eval-audit --csv runs.csv --metrics m1,m2 --target test_accuracy --controls learning_rate,weight_decay,arch --results audit_results.json`

Use MBE for:

- auditing raw metric correlations against controlled partial rank correlation;
- detecting washout or inversion after conditioning on baselines;
- comparing candidate training metrics in a run ledger;
- producing markdown and JSON audit reports for agents and CI pipelines.

Do not use MBE for:

- a single model run;
- generic benchmarking without candidate metrics and controls;
- causal claims without an intervention design;
- proving that a metric is universally reliable.

Problem guides:

- docs/audit_ml_training_metric.md
- docs/agent_recipes.md
- docs/STATISTICAL_ESTIMAND_AND_INFERENCE.md
- docs/CONDITIONAL_METRIC_RELIABILITY_PROTOCOL.md
